# Adamant Protocol

> Adamant is a Layer 1 blockchain protocol designed to combine privacy by default, sub-second finality at design-target validator count, hybrid post-quantum cryptography, phone-verifiable correctness, and credibly neutral governance — with no foundation, no premine, no admin keys, and no upgrade authority after genesis. The protocol uses standard, peer-reviewed cryptographic primitives integrated into a single coherent architecture, with credible neutrality enforced at genesis and the technical architecture chosen to be compatible with that neutrality.

Adamant is currently in specification phase. The whitepaper draft is published for public review. No chain is running yet.

## Key concepts and terminology

The protocol's participation model is four-tiered with bounded power across all tiers:

- **Node Runners** (validators in the whitepaper): operators of the active set running Mysticeti-based DAG consensus and mempool decryption on residential-fibre commodity-desktop hardware. The active set is dynamic — a constitutional floor of 7 validators (the smallest size at which Byzantine fault tolerance retains margin against correlated failures) and a soft ceiling of 75 (calibrated to the throughput floor on residential fibre). Selection is first-come-first-served with persistent membership: validators are admitted in registration order and retain their slot continuously until they fail liveness duties or voluntarily unbond — early committed home-fibre operators cannot be displaced by wealthier latecomers. Each publishes one vertex per 250 ms round, each referencing 2N/3+1 of the prior round. The chain self-activates the moment 7 are simultaneously online; no recruited cohort, no human-in-the-loop activation. At the floor, the chain halts on disagreement rather than forks — safety preserved at the cost of liveness during severe-unavailability periods.
- **Provers** (whitepaper §8.5.3): GPU-class operators producing recursive Halo 2 proofs in a permissionless market. No stake, no application, no approval — provers race to submit valid proofs and win the per-proof bounty (the proof-generation fee dimension). Validators fall back to producing proofs at degraded cadence if the prover market is insufficient; phone-verifiability never depends on the market materialising.
- **Witnesses** (whitepaper §8.7.2): phone-class participants performing four roles — cryptographic attestation, data-availability sampling, recursive-proof verification, and fraud / reordering detection. Permissionless registration with a small Sybil-resistance stake (~100 ADM, 7-day unbonding); slashable for false attestations corroborated by other witnesses. Compensated from a slice of verification fees plus a small slice of issuance.
- **Service Nodes** (whitepaper §9.10): permissionless light-client infrastructure providers. Wallets and applications query them for state via a standard query format. Anyone can run one. Optionally funded by Node Runners from commission revenue (§10.5.5). Fee/ecosystem-funded; receives no issuance.
- **ADM**: the protocol's native token. 100,000,000 ADM genesis pool drained over a five-year launch phase via two acquisition paths. Post-launch issuance is 4% → 3% → 2% → 1% per year, in perpetuity, distributed to Node Runners (majority) and Witnesses (small slice ~5%); provers and service nodes are fee-funded, not issuance-funded. Base fees burned via EIP-1559-style mechanism.
- **Genesis pool**: a fixed 100M ADM supply distributed via burn-to-mint (70M, anyone burning BTC or ETH at a verifiable null address) and Node Runner block rewards (30M). Unclaimed tokens at year 5 are destroyed.
- **Two-regime mempool** (whitepaper §3.6, §3.8, §8.4): at N≥15, threshold encryption with DKG (full MEV resistance). At N<15, time-lock encryption via a Wesolowski VDF on class groups; the rotated round anchor decrypts atomically with their vertex (a bounded intra-anchor reordering surface is honestly disclosed). Hysteresis prevents flapping at the boundary.
- **Security tier** (whitepaper §8.1.7): an on-chain signal indicating the chain's current scale. Tier I (N=7–14, time-lock regime). Tier II (N=15–29, threshold regime). Tier III (N=30+, full design-target). Wallets and applications adapt confirmation requirements accordingly.
- **Hybrid post-quantum posture** (Principle VIII, whitepaper §2.8): ML-DSA (FIPS 204) for identity-binding operations (validator registration, contract deployment, address derivation, opt-in high-value); ML-KEM-768 (FIPS 203) for stealth-address key agreement and encrypted memos — historical privacy survives the quantum threshold. Ed25519 default for ordinary transactions and validator messages (smaller signatures, residential-fiber bandwidth). Trade-off honestly disclosed: ordinary signatures are quantum-forgeable; identity, structural state, and historical privacy are not.
- **Credible neutrality** (Principle I, whitepaper §2.1): the protocol admits no party with the unilateral capability to alter its operation. No on-chain governance, no foundation, no admin keys, no upgrade authority, no premine, no founder allocation. Change requires socially-coordinated forks where Node Runners individually opt in to new client software.

## Design principles

The protocol commits to eight principles in priority order: (1) credible neutrality, (2) privacy by default, (3) verifiability without trust, (4) performance sufficient for use, (5) mutability as a property of objects (not the chain), (6) standard primitives and novel synthesis (no new cryptography), (7) permissionless participation, (8) post-quantum security at identity and privacy layers.

## The seven properties

The protocol's contribution is the systems-level synthesis of seven properties no other chain combines: a 50,000+ TPS floor (not a cap — actual throughput routinely exceeds the floor on better-than-baseline hardware) with sub-second finality at design-target validator count on residential-fibre hardware, programmable privacy (Aztec, 2025), encrypted mempool in production (Shutter, 2024), phone-verifiable correctness (Mina, 2021), native account abstraction (zkSync/StarkNet/Aptos, 2022–24), hybrid post-quantum signatures (NIST FIPS 204/203, 2024), and credibly neutral governance (Bitcoin/Monero, 2009/2014). No single chain in the present landscape combines more than three.

## Pages

- [Home](https://adamantprotocol.com/): Overview of the protocol, the seven properties no chain combines, and the five mechanisms Adamant uses to deliver them.
- [Introduction](https://adamantprotocol.com/about): Plain-English introduction. Four core properties, four-tier participation model, framings against familiar systems, intended users, what the protocol is not, glossary.
- [Genesis](https://adamantprotocol.com/genesis): Launch mechanism, the fixed 100M ADM genesis pool, two acquisition paths, post-launch issuance schedule, what is not happening at launch.
- [Specification](https://adamantprotocol.com/spec): Whitepaper draft in summary form. Reading-friendly index with deep links to the canonical source.

## Whitepaper (canonical source)

The reference whitepaper lives in the protocol repository:

- [Abstract](https://github.com/adamant-protocol/adamant/blob/main/whitepaper/00-abstract.md): One-page summary of the protocol.
- [Introduction & Motivation](https://github.com/adamant-protocol/adamant/blob/main/whitepaper/01-introduction.md): The state of the art in 2026, the gap that remains, and Adamant's thesis.
- [Design Principles](https://github.com/adamant-protocol/adamant/blob/main/whitepaper/02-design-principles.md): The eight principles in priority order.
- [Cryptographic Foundation](https://github.com/adamant-protocol/adamant/blob/main/whitepaper/03-cryptographic-foundation.md): Ed25519, ML-DSA, ML-KEM-768, BLS12-381, SHA-3, Halo 2, threshold encryption, Wesolowski VDF.
- [Identity & Accounts](https://github.com/adamant-protocol/adamant/blob/main/whitepaper/04-identity-accounts.md): Native account abstraction, view keys, selective disclosure.
- [Object Model & State](https://github.com/adamant-protocol/adamant/blob/main/whitepaper/05-object-model-state.md): Object-based state, mutability variants, ownership modes.
- [Execution & VM](https://github.com/adamant-protocol/adamant/blob/main/whitepaper/06-execution-vm.md): Adamant Move language, multi-dimensional gas, mutability annotations.
- [Privacy Layer](https://github.com/adamant-protocol/adamant/blob/main/whitepaper/07-privacy-layer.md): Halo 2 circuits, shielded transactions, view keys, ML-KEM-based stealth addresses.
- [Consensus](https://github.com/adamant-protocol/adamant/blob/main/whitepaper/08-consensus.md): Mysticeti-based DAG, dynamic active set with floor and ceiling, two-regime mempool, recursive proofs with prover market and validator fallback, witness tier, security tier disclosure.
- [Networking & Mempool](https://github.com/adamant-protocol/adamant/blob/main/whitepaper/09-networking-mempool.md): libp2p networking, encrypted mempool, service-node infrastructure market.
- [Economics & Incentives](https://github.com/adamant-protocol/adamant/blob/main/whitepaper/10-economics-incentives.md): Genesis pool, post-launch issuance, fee burn, prover bounty, witness compensation, validator-funded infrastructure.
- [Genesis & Constitution](https://github.com/adamant-protocol/adamant/blob/main/whitepaper/11-genesis-constitution.md): What is fixed at genesis. The structural absence of any mechanism to modify the protocol.
- [Conclusion & Open Problems](https://github.com/adamant-protocol/adamant/blob/main/whitepaper/12-conclusion.md): What remains to be done, acknowledged constraints.

## Repository

- [GitHub: adamant-protocol/adamant](https://github.com/adamant-protocol/adamant): Reference implementation. Apache 2.0. The implementation is forthcoming; the protocol is in specification phase.
- [GitHub: adamant-protocol/adamant-website](https://github.com/adamant-protocol/adamant-website): Source code for this website.

- [Updates](https://adamantprotocol.com/updates): Project updates and design notes.
